name: cattrs

metadata.name: cattrs
metadata.summary: Composable complex class support for attrs and dataclasses.

documentation:
# cattrs

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/cattrs.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/cattrs
```

```{image} https://github.com/python-attrs/cattrs/workflows/CI/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/python-attrs/cattrs/actions?workflow=CI
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/cattrs/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://cattrs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/cattrs.svg
:alt: Supported Python versions
:target: https://github.com/python-attrs/cattrs
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/python-attrs/cattrs/branch/master/graph/badge.svg
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/python-attrs/cattrs/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:target: https://github.com/ambv/black
```

______________________________________________________________________

`cattrs` is an open source Python library for structuring and unstructuring
data. `cattrs` works best with `attrs` classes, dataclasses and the usual
Python collections, but other kinds of classes are supported by manually
registering converters.

Python has a rich set of powerful, easy to use, built-in data types like
dictionaries, lists and tuples. These data types are also the lingua franca
of most data serialization libraries, for formats like json, msgpack, yaml or
toml.

Data types like this, and mappings like `dict` s in particular, represent
unstructured data. Your data is, in all likelihood, structured: not all
combinations of field names or values are valid inputs to your programs. In
Python, structured data is better represented with classes and enumerations.
`attrs` is an excellent library for declaratively describing the structure of
your data, and validating it.

When you're handed unstructured data (by your network, file system, database...),
`cattrs` helps to convert this data into structured data. When you have to
convert your structured data into data types other libraries can handle,
`cattrs` turns your classes and enumerations into dictionaries, integers and
strings.

Here's a simple taste. The list containing a float, an int and a string
gets converted into a tuple of three ints.

```pycon
>>> import cattrs
>>>
>>> cattrs.structure([1.0, 2, "3"], tuple[int, int, int])
(1, 2, 3)
```

`cattrs` works well with `attrs` classes out of the box.

```pycon
>>> from attrs import frozen
>>> import cattrs
>>>
>>> @frozen  # It works with non-frozen classes too.
... class C:
...     a: int
...     b: str
...
>>> instance = C(1, 'a')
>>> cattrs.unstructure(instance)
{'a': 1, 'b': 'a'}
>>> cattrs.structure({'a': 1, 'b': 'a'}, C)
C(a=1, b='a')
```

Here's a much more complex example, involving `attrs` classes with type
metadata.

```pycon
>>> from enum import unique, Enum
>>> from typing import Optional, Sequence, Union
>>> from cattrs import structure, unstructure
>>> from attrs import define, field
>>>
>>> @unique
... class CatBreed(Enum):
...     SIAMESE = "siamese"
...     MAINE_COON = "maine_coon"
...     SACRED_BIRMAN = "birman"
...
>>> @define
... class Cat:
...     breed: CatBreed
...     names: Sequence[str]
...
>>> @define
... class DogMicrochip:
...     chip_id = field()  # Type annotations are optional, but recommended
...     time_chipped: float = field()
...
>>> @define
... class Dog:
...     cuteness: int
...     chip: Optional[DogMicrochip] = None
...
>>> p = unstructure([Dog(cuteness=1, chip=DogMicrochip(chip_id=1, time_chipped=10.0)),
...                  Cat(breed=CatBreed.MAINE_COON, names=('Fluffly', 'Fluffer'))])
...
>>> print(p)
[{'cuteness': 1, 'chip': {'chip_id': 1, 'time_chipped': 10.0}}, {'breed': 'maine_coon', 'names': ('Fluffly', 'Fluffer')}]
>>> print(structure(p, list[Union[Dog, Cat]]))
[Dog(cuteness=1, chip=DogMicrochip(chip_id=1, time_chipped=10.0)), Cat(breed=<CatBreed.MAINE_COON: 'maine_coon'>, names=['Fluffly', 'Fluffer'])]
```

Consider unstructured data a low-level representation that needs to be converted
to structured data to be handled, and use `structure`. When you're done,
`unstructure` the data to its unstructured form and pass it along to another
library or module. Use [attrs type metadata](http://attrs.readthedocs.io/en/stable/examples.html#types)
to add type metadata to attributes, so `cattrs` will know how to structure and
destructure them.

- Free software: MIT license
- Documentation: <https://cattrs.readthedocs.io>.
- Python versions supported: 3.7 and up. (Older Python versions, like 2.7, 3.5 and 3.6 are supported by older versions; see the changelog.)

## Features

- Converts structured data into unstructured data, recursively:

  - `attrs` classes and dataclasses are converted into dictionaries in a way similar to `attrs.asdict`, or into tuples in a way similar to `attrs.astuple`.
  - Enumeration instances are converted to their values.
  - Other types are let through without conversion. This includes types such as
    integers, dictionaries, lists and instances of non-`attrs` classes.
  - Custom converters for any type can be registered using `register_unstructure_hook`.

- Converts unstructured data into structured data, recursively, according to
  your specification given as a type. The following types are supported:

  - `typing.Optional[T]`.

  - `typing.List[T]`, `typing.MutableSequence[T]`, `typing.Sequence[T]` (converts to a list).

  - `typing.Tuple` (both variants, `Tuple[T, ...]` and `Tuple[X, Y, Z]`).

  - `typing.MutableSet[T]`, `typing.Set[T]` (converts to a set).

  - `typing.FrozenSet[T]` (converts to a frozenset).

  - `typing.Dict[K, V]`, `typing.MutableMapping[K, V]`, `typing.Mapping[K, V]` (converts to a dict).

  - `attrs` classes with simple attributes and the usual `__init__`.

    - Simple attributes are attributes that can be assigned unstructured data,
      like numbers, strings, and collections of unstructured data.

  - All `attrs` classes and dataclasses with the usual `__init__`, if their complex attributes have type metadata.

  - `typing.Union` s of supported `attrs` classes, given that all of the classes have a unique field.

  - `typing.Union` s of anything, given that you provide a disambiguation function for it.

  - Custom converters for any type can be registered using `register_structure_hook`.

`cattrs` comes with preconfigured converters for a number of serialization libraries, including json, msgpack, bson, yaml and toml.
For details, see the [cattr.preconf package](https://cattrs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/preconf.html).

## Additional documentation

- [On structured and unstructured data, or the case for cattrs](https://threeofwands.com/on-structured-and-unstructured-data-or-the-case-for-cattrs/)
- [Why I use attrs instead of pydantic](https://threeofwands.com/why-i-use-attrs-instead-of-pydantic/)
- [cattrs I: un/structuring speed](https://threeofwands.com/why-cattrs-is-so-fast/)

## Credits

Major credits to Hynek Schlawack for creating [attrs] and its predecessor,
[characteristic].

`cattrs` is tested with [Hypothesis], by David R. MacIver.

`cattrs` is benchmarked using [perf] and [pytest-benchmark].

This package was created with [Cookiecutter] and the [audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage] project template.

[attrs]: https://github.com/hynek/attrs
[audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage]: https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage
[characteristic]: https://github.com/hynek/characteristic
[cookiecutter]: https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter
[hypothesis]: http://hypothesis.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
[perf]: https://github.com/haypo/perf
[pytest-benchmark]: https://pytest-benchmark.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.msgpack
documentation: Preconfigured converters for msgpack.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.msgpack.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the msgpack library.

* datetimes are serialized as timestamp floats
* sets are serialized as lists

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.ujson
documentation: Preconfigured converters for ujson.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.ujson.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the ujson library.

* bytes are serialized as base64 strings
* datetimes are serialized as ISO 8601
* sets are serialized as lists

classes.name: cattrs.errors.StructureHandlerNotFoundError
documentation: Error raised when structuring cannot find a handler for converting inputs into {attr}`type_`.

classes.name: cattrs.errors.IterableValidationError
documentation: Raised when structuring an iterable.

classes.name: cattrs.errors.ClassValidationError
documentation: Raised when validating a class if any attributes are invalid.

classes.name: cattrs.errors.ForbiddenExtraKeysError
documentation:
Raised when `forbid_extra_keys` is activated and such extra keys are detected during structuring.

The attribute `extra_fields` is a sequence of those extra keys, which were the cause of this error,
and `cl` is the class which was structured with those extra keys.

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.json
documentation: Preconfigured converters for the stdlib json.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.json.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the stdlib json module.

* bytes are serialized as base64 strings
* datetimes are serialized as ISO 8601
* counters are serialized as dicts
* sets are serialized as lists

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.pyyaml
documentation: Preconfigured converters for pyyaml.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.pyyaml.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the pyyaml library.

* frozensets are serialized as lists
* string enums are converted into strings explicitly

modules.name: cattrs.disambiguators
documentation: Utilities for union (sum type) disambiguation.

functions.name: cattrs.disambiguators.create_uniq_field_dis_func
documentation:
Given attr classes, generate a disambiguation function.

The function is based on unique fields.

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.orjson
documentation: Preconfigured converters for orjson.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.orjson.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the orjson library.

* bytes are serialized as base85 strings
* datetimes are serialized as ISO 8601
* sets are serialized as lists
* string enum mapping keys have special handling
* mapping keys are coerced into strings when unstructuring

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.bson
documentation: Preconfigured converters for bson.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.bson.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the bson library.

* sets are serialized as lists
* byte mapping keys are base85-encoded into strings when unstructuring, and reverse
* non-string, non-byte mapping keys are coerced into strings when unstructuring
* a deserialization hook is registered for bson.ObjectId by default

classes.name: cattrs.preconf.bson.Base85Bytes
documentation: A subclass to help with binary key encoding/decoding.

classes.name: cattrs.dispatch._DispatchNotFound
documentation: A dummy object to help signify a dispatch not found.

classes.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch.register_cls_list
documentation: Register a class to direct or singledispatch.

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch.register_func_list
documentation:
Register a predicate function to determine if the handle
should be used for the type.

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch.clear_direct
documentation: Clear the direct dispatch.

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch.clear_cache
documentation: Clear all caches.

MultiStrategyDispatch uses a combination of exact-match dispatch,
singledispatch, and FunctionDispatch.

classes.name: cattrs.dispatch.FunctionDispatch

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.FunctionDispatch.dispatch
documentation: returns the appropriate handler, for the object passed.

FunctionDispatch is similar to functools.singledispatch, but
instead dispatches based on functions that take the type of the
first argument in the method, and return True or False.

objects that help determine dispatch should be instantiated objects.

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.tomlkit
documentation: Preconfigured converters for tomlkit.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.tomlkit.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the tomlkit library.

* bytes are serialized as base85 strings
* sets are serialized as lists
* tuples are serializas as lists
* mapping keys are coerced into strings when unstructuring

functions.name: cattrs._compat.has_with_generic
documentation: Test whether the class if a normal or generic attrs or dataclass.

functions.name: cattrs._compat.adapted_fields
documentation: Return the attrs format of `fields()` for attrs and dataclasses.

functions.name: cattrs._compat.is_literal
documentation: No literals in 3.7.

functions.name: cattrs._compat.copy_with
documentation: Replace a generic type's arguments.

functions.name: cattrs._compat.copy_with
documentation: Replace a generic type's arguments.

functions.name: cattrs.gen.make_dict_unstructure_fn
documentation:
Generate a specialized dict unstructuring function for an attrs class or a
dataclass.

functions.name: cattrs.gen.make_dict_structure_fn
documentation: Generate a specialized dict structuring function for an attrs class.

functions.name: cattrs.gen.make_iterable_unstructure_fn
documentation: Generate a specialized unstructure function for an iterable.

functions.name: cattrs.gen.make_hetero_tuple_unstructure_fn
documentation: Generate a specialized unstructure function for a heterogenous tuple.

functions.name: cattrs.gen.make_mapping_unstructure_fn
documentation: Generate a specialized unstructure function for a mapping.

functions.name: cattrs.gen.make_mapping_structure_fn
documentation: Generate a specialized unstructure function for a mapping.

functions.name: cattrs.gen._generate_unique_filename
documentation: Create a "filename" suitable for a function being generated.

functions.name: cattrs.converters._subclass
documentation: a shortcut

classes.name: cattrs.converters.UnstructureStrategy
documentation: `attrs` classes unstructuring strategies.

classes.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.unstruct_strat
documentation: The default way of unstructuring `attrs` classes.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_unstructure_hook
documentation:
Register a class-to-primitive converter function for a class.

The converter function should take an instance of the class and return
its Python equivalent.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_unstructure_hook_func
documentation:
Register a class-to-primitive converter function for a class, using
a function to check if it's a match.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_unstructure_hook_factory
documentation:
Register a hook factory for a given predicate.

A predicate is a function that, given a type, returns whether the factory
can produce a hook for that type.

A factory is a callable that, given a type, produces an unstructuring
hook for that type. This unstructuring hook will be cached.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_structure_hook
documentation:
Register a primitive-to-class converter function for a type.

The converter function should take two arguments:
  * a Python object to be converted,
  * the type to convert to

and return the instance of the class. The type may seem redundant, but
is sometimes needed (for example, when dealing with generic classes).

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_structure_hook_func
documentation:
Register a class-to-primitive converter function for a class, using
a function to check if it's a match.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_structure_hook_factory
documentation:
Register a hook factory for a given predicate.

A predicate is a function that, given a type, returns whether the factory
can produce a hook for that type.

A factory is a callable that, given a type, produces a structuring
hook for that type. This structuring hook will be cached.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.structure
documentation: Convert unstructured Python data structures to structured data.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.unstructure_attrs_asdict
documentation: Our version of `attrs.asdict`, so we can call back to us.
Classes to Python primitives.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.unstructure_attrs_astuple
documentation: Our version of `attrs.astuple`, so we can call back to us.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_enum
documentation: Convert an enum to its value.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_identity
documentation: Just pass it through.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_seq
documentation: Convert a sequence to primitive equivalents.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_mapping
documentation: Convert a mapping of attr classes to primitive equivalents.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_union
documentation:
Unstructure an object as a union.

By default, just unstructures the instance.
note: Use UnionType when 3.11 is released as
the behaviour of @final is changed. This would
affect how we can support UnionType in ._compat.py

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_error
documentation: At the bottom of the condition stack, we explode if we can't handle it.
Python primitives to classes.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._gen_structure_generic
documentation: Create and return a hook for structuring generics.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._gen_attrs_union_structure
documentation: Generate a structuring function for a union of attrs classes (and maybe None).

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_call
documentation:
Just call `cl` with the given `obj`.

This is just an optimization on the `_structure_default` case, when
we know we can skip the `if` s. Use for `str`, `bytes`, `enum`,
etc.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.structure_attrs_fromtuple
documentation: Load an attrs class from a sequence (tuple).
Attrs classes.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_attribute
documentation: Handle an individual attrs attribute.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.structure_attrs_fromdict
documentation: Instantiate an attrs class from a mapping (dict).

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_list
documentation: Convert an iterable to a potentially generic list.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_set
documentation: Convert an iterable into a potentially generic set.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_frozenset
documentation: Convert an iterable into a potentially generic frozenset.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_dict
documentation: Convert a mapping into a potentially generic dict.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_union
documentation: Deal with structuring a union.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_tuple
documentation: Deal with structuring into a tuple.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._get_dis_func
documentation: Fetch or try creating a disambiguation function for a union.
Converts between structured and unstructured data.

classes.name: cattrs.converters.Converter
documentation: A converter which generates specialized un/structuring functions.
